sourceMovie.title: Iron Lung
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Iron Lung works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Thing
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Iron Lung clicked for you, The Thing (1982) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Iron Lung?
faq.answer: Try The Thing, Event Horizon, Lifeforce—each shares DNA with Iron Lung while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Iron Lung?
faq.answer: Iron Lung (2026) is best described as Horror, Science Fiction, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Iron Lung worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
